# OHLC

<TierBadge tier="value" />

Fetch intraday OHLC bars for a stock on a single date.

- Aggregated OHLC bars that use [SIP rules](https://docs.thetadata.us/Articles/Data-And-Requests/OHLC-EOD.html) for each bar. Time timestamp of the bar represents the opening time of the bar. For a trade to be part of the bar:  `bar time` <= `trade time` < `bar timestamp + ivl`, where ivl is the specified interval size in milliseconds.
- Set the `venue` parameter to `nqb` to access current-day real-time historic data from the [Nasdaq Basic feed](https://docs.thetadata.us/Articles/Data-And-Requests/The-SIPs) if the account has a [stocks standard or pro subscription](https://www.thetadata.net/subscribe.html#stocks).
- Multi-day requests are limited to 1 month of data.

<SdkTabs>

<template #rust>

```rust
pub fn stock_history_ohlc(&self, symbol: &str, date: &str) -> StockHistoryOhlcBuilder<'_>
```

Optional parameters chain on the builder: `.interval(&str)`, `.start_time(&str)`, `.end_time(&str)`, `.venue(&str)`, `.start_date(&str)`, `.end_date(&str)`. Execute with `.await` â†’ `Result<Vec<OhlcTick>, Error>`, or decode chunk-by-chunk with `.stream(handler)`.

**Example**

```rust
let rows = tdx
    .stock_history_ohlc("AAPL", "20250303")
    .interval("1m")
    .await?;
for t in &rows {
    println!("date={} open={} high={} low={} close={}", t.date, t.open, t.high, t.low, t.close);
}
```

</template>

<template #python>

```python
ThetaDataDxClient.stock_history_ohlc(
    symbol, date,
    *,
    interval=None, start_time=None, end_time=None, venue=None, start_date=None,
    end_date=None, timeout_ms=None,
) -> OhlcTickList
```

`stock_history_ohlc_async(...)` awaits the same call shape. Chain `.to_pandas()` / `.to_polars()` / `.to_arrow()` / `.to_list()` on the result.

**Example**

```python
rows = tdx.stock_history_ohlc("AAPL", "20250303", interval="1m")
for t in rows:
    print(t.date, t.open, t.high, t.low, t.close)
```

</template>

<template #typescript>

```typescript
stockHistoryOHLC(
  symbol: string, date: string | Date, options?: { ... },
): Array<OhlcTick>
```

Optional parameters ride in a single trailing options object: `interval?: string`, `startTime?: string | Date`, `endTime?: string | Date`, `venue?: string`, `startDate?: string | Date`, `endDate?: string | Date`, `timeoutMs?: number`.

**Example**

```typescript
const rows = tdx.stockHistoryOHLC('AAPL', '20250303', { interval: '1m' });
for (const t of rows) {
  console.log(t.date, t.open, t.high, t.low, t.close);
}
```

</template>

<template #cpp>

```cpp
std::vector<OhlcTick> stock_history_ohlc(
    const std::string& symbol,
    const std::string& date,
    const EndpointRequestOptions& options = {}) const;
```

Optional parameters chain on `EndpointRequestOptions`: `.with_interval(...)`, `.with_start_time(...)`, `.with_end_time(...)`, `.with_venue(...)`, `.with_start_date(...)`, `.with_end_date(...)`. Throws `tdx::Error` on failure.

**Example**

```cpp
auto rows = client.stock_history_ohlc("AAPL", "20250303",
    tdx::EndpointRequestOptions{}.with_interval("1m"));
for (const auto& t : rows) {
    std::cout << t.date << ' ' << t.open << ' ' << t.high << ' ' << t.low << ' ' << t.close << "\n";
}
```

</template>

<template #http>

```http
GET http://127.0.0.1:25503/v3/stock/history/ohlc
```

Served by the bundled [server binary](/server/); responses stream as JSON, CSV, or NDJSON via the `format` parameter.

**Example**

```bash
curl 'http://127.0.0.1:25503/v3/stock/history/ohlc?symbol=AAPL&date=2025-03-03&interval=1m'
```

</template>

</SdkTabs>

## Parameters

| Name | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `symbol` | string | yes | â€” | Ticker symbol (e.g. AAPL) |
| `date` | date | yes | â€” | Date YYYYMMDD |
| `interval` | string | no | `1s` | Interval preset or millisecond string. Defaults to `1s` when omitted â€” matching the upstream ThetaData Python library. Accepted values: `tick`, `10ms`, `100ms`, `500ms`, `1s`, `5s`, `10s`, `15s`, `30s`, `1m`, `5m`, `10m`, `15m`, `30m`, `1h`. |
| `start_time` | string | no | `09:30:00` | Start time filter |
| `end_time` | string | no | `16:00:00` | End time filter |
| `venue` | string | no | `nqb` | Venue/exchange filter. Accepted values: `nqb`, `utp_cta`. |
| `start_date` | date | no | â€” | Start date YYYYMMDD |
| `end_date` | date | no | â€” | End date YYYYMMDD |
| `timeout_ms` | int | no | â€” | Per-request deadline in milliseconds. 0 means no deadline. |

## Response

Rows of `OhlcTick`:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ms_of_day` | i32 | Opening time of the bar, milliseconds since midnight ET. |
| `open` | f64 | Opening trade price. |
| `high` | f64 | Highest traded price. |
| `low` | f64 | Lowest traded price. |
| `close` | f64 | Closing traded price. |
| `volume` | i64 | Number of contracts or shares traded. |
| `count` | i64 | Number of trades. |
| `vwap` | f64 | Volume-weighted average price of the session. |
| `date` | i32 | Trading date as a YYYYMMDD integer. |
